attacking_style: Relies on tactical positioning and bonus second sprints rather than explosive long-range attacks. Targets intermediate bonifications and stage finishes with calculated efforts, showing patience over aggression.
team_tactics: Limited evidence of dedicated team setup; appears to operate with relative independence, using positioning and technical skills (descending) to gain time rather than domestique-led trains.
terrain_preference: Technical descents are a key weapon for gaining time. Handles stage race climbs with mature pacing that improves through efforts rather than fading. Suited to medium-mountain stages where technical skills and positioning matter.
vulnerability: Limited observations of response to sustained high-intensity attacks or explosive summit finishes. Bonus-second dependency suggests potential weakness in pure climbing power against elite Grand Tour climbers.
key_patterns: Actively hunts bonus seconds at intermediates and finishes to gain GC time (3 observations)
Superior technical descending used as tactical weapon to chase/gain time (2 observations)
Exponential pacing strategy: strengthens through climbs rather than fading, showing maturity (2 observations)
Tactical awareness beyond typical neo-pro: calculates time gaps and opportunities strategically (3 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: demonstrated progressive maturity throughout Tour de Suisse 2023, with smart tactical racing, technical prowess, and pacing discipline suggesting upward development curve
